name: Boomerang / Chevron
description: A broad V-shaped or boomerang-shaped craft, typically 200–1000 feet across. Five to ten lights positioned at the apex and along the trailing edges in amber or white. Always silent. Appears as a V-formation of lights in night sky — hull is only detectable by star occlusion. Moves slowly, sometimes hovering. The Phoenix Lights variant is mile-wide. The Hudson Valley variant appeared nightly for years.

ir_signature.ir_notes: Night sightings only in canonical cases. Hull visible only by star-occlusion — blocks stars cleanly suggesting solid body at altitude.

cultural_context: The Phoenix Lights of March 13 1997 — witnessed from Henderson Nevada to Tucson Arizona by thousands including the governor of Arizona — remain the most widely witnessed UFO event in American history. The V-formation of amber lights, mile-wide, silent, 100 feet overhead. Governor Fife Symington called a press conference the next day... bringing out an aide in an alien costume. He later admitted he'd also seen it and was shaken by it.
